description_long: This is a tall, multi-story stone tower with a classical architectural design, featuring three main levels and a domed roof. The lower level has a series of large, arched openings that suggest an entrance or open gallery, while the middle and upper levels are adorned with arched windows and columns. The structure is predominantly gray, with subtle weathering and moss or plant growth at the base, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The dome at the top is smooth and slightly weathered, sitting atop a colonnaded drum. The tower's overall form is symmetrical, with each level slightly recessed from the one below, creating a tiered effect. Decorative moldings and arches add to the historic and monumental appearance of the building.
description: A tall, gray stone tower with arched windows, a domed roof, and classical architectural details. Weathered surfaces and plant growth at the base suggest age and grandeur.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entrance and symmetrical arrangement of windows and columns.
description_view_1: Similar to the front, but with slight differences in plant growth and weathering at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with fewer visible decorative elements and less pronounced plant growth.
description_view_3: Side view showing the same tiered structure, with minor variations in base details and weathering.
